title: quercus infectoria
intro: Quercus infectoria, also known as galls of oak, is a plant that belongs to the family Fagaceae, specifically a type of oak tree gall formed by the larvae of the wasp Cynipidae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus infectoria include its ability to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to protect against cancer and cardiovascular disease. Quercus infectoria is typically prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote health and well-being.
